frameworks.name: fMRI adaptation
frameworks.one_line: BOLD reduction after prolonged stimulus reveals subvoxel neuronal selectivity

frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis (MVPA)
frameworks.one_line: multivariate statistics recovers subvoxel selectivity biases across voxels

core_question: How do fMRI adaptation and MVPA compare in their sensitivity for probing subvoxel neuronal selectivity in visual cortex?
key_assumptions: voxels contain heterogeneous neuronal populations not resolvable at mm scale
adaptation-induced BOLD reduction reflects stimulus-selective neuronal subpopulations
small per-voxel response biases carry information recoverable by multivariate decoders
manipulations: method used to probe subvoxel selectivity (adaptation vs MVPA)
stimulus characteristics targeted in visual cortex
key_findings: both techniques were applied within the same study to visual cortex
the two methods had not previously been compared explicitly under matched conditions
interpretations: direct comparison clarifies the relative sensitivities of adaptation and MVPA for subvoxel inference
